heading: Federal Land Disposal Account
label: 2305 Federal Land Disposal Account

children.heading: Deposit of proceeds
children.label: a Deposit of proceeds
children.text: Notwithstanding any other law (except a law that specifically provides for a proportion of the proceeds to be distributed to any trust funds of any States), the gross proceeds of the sale or exchange of public land under this chapter shall be deposited in a separate account in the Treasury of the United States to be known as the “Federal Land Disposal Account”.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Availability
children.label: b Availability
children.text: Amounts in the Federal Land Disposal Account shall be available to the Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture, without further Act of appropriation, to carry out this chapter.

children.heading: Use of the Federal Land Disposal Account
children.label: c Use of the Federal Land Disposal Account

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Funds in the Federal Land Disposal Account shall be expended in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: inholdings;

children.label: ii
children.text: adjacent to federally designated areas and contain exceptional resources; or

children.label: iii
children.text: adjacent to inaccessible lands open to public hunting, fishing, recreational shooting, or other recreational purposes.

children.heading: .—Inholdings
children.label: B .—Inholdings
children.text: Not less than 80 percent of the funds allocated for the purchase of land within each State shall be used to acquire inholdings identified under .section 2303 of this title

children.heading: .—Administrative and other expenses
children.label: C .—Administrative and other expenses
children.text: An amount not to exceed 20 percent of the funds deposited in the Federal Land Disposal Account may be used by the Secretary for administrative and other expenses necessary to carry out the land disposal program under .section 2304 of this title

children.heading: .—Same state purchases
children.label: D .—Same state purchases
children.text: Of the amounts not used under subparagraph (C), not less than 80 percent shall be expended within the State in which the funds were generated. Any remaining funds may be expended in any other State.

children.label: E
children.text: Any funds made available under subparagraph (D) that are not obligated or expended by the end of the fourth full fiscal year after the date of the sale or exchange of land that generated the funds may be expended in any State.

children.label: A
children.text: the date the inholding was established (as provided in );section 2303(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the acquisition of the land or interest therein will increase the public availability of resources for, and facilitate public access to, hunting, fishing, and other recreational activities;

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which acquisition of the land or interest therein will facilitate management efficiency; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other criteria as the Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture deem appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: from a willing seller;

children.label: B
children.text: contingent on the conveyance of title acceptable to the Secretary, or the Secretary of Agriculture in the case of an acquisition of National Forest System land, using title standards of the Attorney General;

children.label: C
children.text: at a price not to exceed fair market value consistent with applicable provisions of the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions; and

children.label: D
children.text: managed as part of the unit within which it is contained.

children.heading: Contaminated sites and sites difficult and uneconomic to manage
children.label: d Contaminated sites and sites difficult and uneconomic to manage

children.text: contains a hazardous substance or is otherwise contaminated; or
because of the location or other characteristics of the land, would be difficult or uneconomic to manage as Federal land.

children.heading: Chapter 2003 of title 54
children.label: e Chapter 2003 of title 54
children.text: Funds made available under this section shall be supplemental to any funds appropriated under chapter 2003 of title 54.
